# Tasks: TUI Component Catalog — CC → KOSMOS Verdict Matrix + Brand-System Doctrine

**Input**: Design documents from `/specs/034-tui-component-catalog/`
**Prerequisites**: `plan.md` (required), `spec.md` (required for user stories), `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/` (5 files), `quickstart.md`
**Tests**: No pytest additions — `/speckit-analyze` consumes the `contracts/*.md` invariants (I3–I17, BSS-01..BSS-09, AG-01..AG-06) as lint rules. Grep CI gate test fixtures are generated by a single Task; workflow itself is deferred per FR-011.

**Organization**: Tasks grouped by user story (US1–US6) from `spec.md`. Every story is independently deliverable and testable.

## Format: `[ID] [P?] [Story] Description`

- **[P]**: Can run in parallel (different files / different subdirectory partitions, no shared-write conflicts)
- **[Story]**: Maps task to spec.md user story (US1–US6)
- All file paths are repository-root-relative; KOSMOS repo root is the working directory

## Path Conventions

- **New docs**: `docs/tui/component-catalog.md`, `docs/tui/accessibility-gate.md`, `docs/design/brand-system.md`
- **Modified source**: `tui/src/theme/tokens.ts` (type surface only; FR-030)
- **Spec artifacts consumed by `/speckit-analyze`**: `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/contracts/*.md` (already authored in Phase 1)

---

## Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

**Purpose**: Pin the enumeration snapshot, prepare directory scaffolds, and capture the inputs every downstream Task depends on.

- [X] T001 Create `docs/tui/` directory and verify `docs/design/` exists; add no README (scope discipline per FR-030)
- [X] T002 Capture CC sourcemap enumeration snapshot: run `find .references/claude-code-sourcemap/restored-src/src/components -type f \( -name "*.tsx" -o -name "*.ts" \) | sort` and persist output to `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/cc-file-list.txt` (scratch, not committed to docs/); verify count == 389 and submodule HEAD == `a8a678c` (evidence for catalog header per catalog-row-schema.md §1)
- [X] T003 [P] Inventory current `tui/src/theme/tokens.ts` type-surface identifiers to `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/existing-tokens.txt` (scratch) for BAN-01..BAN-07 allow-list bootstrap (grep-gate-rules.md §3)
- [X] T004 [P] Re-verify Epic state via `gh api graphql` per AGENTS.md GraphQL-only rule: confirm M #1310 OPEN, B #1297 CLOSED, A #1298 CLOSED, C/D/E/H/I/J/K/L OPEN; record output to `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/epic-state-2026-04-20.json` (scratch)

## Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

**Purpose**: Reproducible family classification and reference indexes that ALL user stories depend on. No user story may begin until T005–T008 complete.

**⚠️ CRITICAL**: US1 catalog authoring (Phase 3) requires T005 + T006; US4 accessibility rows (Phase 6) require T005; US6 DISCARD evidence audit (Phase 8) requires T006.

- [X] T005 Partition the 389-file list into 31 subdirectory families + root-level semantic bins (`root.logo-wordmark`, `root.dialogs`, `root.shortcuts`, `root.dev-ui`, `root.onboarding`, `root.misc`) per catalog-row-schema.md §2.1; write partition map to `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/family-partition.md` (scratch) with per-family file counts summing to 389
- [X] T006 Build the ADR-006 Part D reference index: enumerate every Part D-1 exclusion category (dev-only slash commands, Anthropic-platform surfaces, migration helpers, domain-mismatch modules) and every Part D-3 KOSMOS-original carve-out into `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/adr-006-evidence-index.md` (scratch) — drives DISCARD rationale citations per FR-004
- [X] T007 Build the owning-Epic lookup table from `data-model.md § 1.4` + research.md § 2.3 into `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/owning-epic-map.md` (scratch) — columns: id, issue, state, closure_delegate; drives FR-003 + FR-026 + research §R3 compliance
- [X] T008 Build the KOSMOS current-tree index at `tui/src/components/` (26 files across 4 subdirs per research §2.2) into `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/kosmos-tree-map.md` (scratch) — drives `KOSMOS target` column for PORT/REWRITE rows

**Checkpoint**: Foundation ready — US1/US2/US3 authoring may start in parallel; US4 waits on US1 row list; US5/US6 wait on US1 completion.

---

## Phase 3: User Story 1 — Future-Epic Author Reads a Single Verdict (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Deliver `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` — the 389-row authoritative verdict matrix consumed by every downstream Epic (B/C/D/E/H/I/J/K/L) spec input.

**Independent Test**: A reviewer opens `docs/tui/component-catalog.md`, picks any of the 389 CC component files at random, and finds exactly one row with verdict, owning Epic (if not DISCARD), CC source path, KOSMOS target path (if PORT/REWRITE), and evidence citation. No file yields zero or multiple verdicts (SC-001).

### Implementation for User Story 1

- [X] T009 [US1] Author `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` header per catalog-row-schema.md §1 (Epic, branch, commit, CC sourcemap commit `a8a678c`, file count 389, subdirectory count 31, recount command evidence, 389-vs-286 discrepancy note FR-007, downstream-Epic contract line FR-028)
- [X] T010 [P] [US1] Classify the 8 design-system / chrome families (`design-system/`, `ui/`, `Spinner/`, `LogoV2/`, `HighlightedCode/`, `StructuredDiff/`, `diff/`, `CustomSelect/`) and emit catalog rows to `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` — verdict + owning Epic + KOSMOS target + rationale + evidence per FR-002/003/006
- [X] T011 [P] [US1] Classify the 7 conversation / IO families (`messages/`, `PromptInput/`, `HelpV2/`, `hooks/`, `memory/`, `shell/`, `ClaudeCodeHint/`) and emit catalog rows — apply Epic E #1300 (IME) and Epic D #1299 (context assembly) owning where applicable per FR-003
- [X] T012 [P] [US1] Classify the 8 permission / safety / trust families (`permissions/`, `TrustDialog/`, `ManagedSettingsSecurityDialog/`, `sandbox/`, `Passes/`, `agents/`, `teams/`, `grove/`) and emit catalog rows — apply closed-Epic rule for `permissions/*` owned by B #1297 per research §R3 (some → PORT "implementation complete via #1441"; remaining TUI rewrites → REWRITE with `closure_delegate = M #1310`)
- [X] T013 [P] [US1] Classify the 8 tooling / integration families (`mcp/`, `skills/`, `tasks/`, `wizard/`, `DesktopUpsell/`, `FeedbackSurvey/`, `LspRecommendation/`, `Settings/`) and emit catalog rows — Settings family flags Epic K #1308 ownership
- [X] T014 [US1] Classify the 113 root-level files via the 6 semantic bins built in T005 (`root.logo-wordmark`, `root.dialogs`, `root.shortcuts`, `root.dev-ui`, `root.onboarding`, `root.misc`) and emit catalog rows — apply ADR-006 Part D-1 DISCARDs for dev-only slash commands + Anthropic-platform surfaces (AutoUpdater.tsx, claudeAiLimits, oauth, autoDream, PromptSuggestion, MagicDocs, upstreamproxy, bridge, buddy) with Rationale starting `ADR-006 Part D-1` per FR-004
- [X] T015 [US1] Apply FR-027 aggregation rule to any family with > 10 REWRITE rows: collapse into one aggregated row (`<family>/*`, `Files = N`, constituent file list in Rationale); update catalog body in place to keep file-count invariant I3 (sum == 389) satisfied
- [X] T016 [US1] Pre-flight sub-issue budget audit per research §R4 partition (≤ 40 REWRITE + ≤ 30 family-batch + ≤ 10 DEFER + ≤ 10 process Tasks); if projected Epic-M sub-issue count > 85, escalate aggregation per FR-027 until count ≤ 85 (5-slot buffer before 90-cap); record final projection in `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/sub-issue-budget.md` (scratch)
- [X] T017 [US1] Append the Downstream Epic spec-input checklist appendix to `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` per catalog-row-schema.md §4 + FR-029 (≤ 5 bullets per SC-010)
- [X] T018 [US1] Validate `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` against catalog-row-schema.md §3 invariants I3–I6 (file-count sum == 389, DISCARD rationale prefix, DEFER "unblock when" string, PORT/REWRITE accessibility_gate_ref non-empty); fix in place; document validation run in the catalog trailer

**Checkpoint**: MVP delivered — catalog alone satisfies User Story 1's Independent Test. Epic can ship here if US2–US6 are deferred.

## Phase 4: User Story 2 — Brand Guardian Enforces Token-Naming Doctrine (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Deliver the machine-checkable token naming doctrine + type-surface compliance so a Brand Guardian can reject bad tokens by citing a single §2 subsection (SC-012).

**Independent Test**: Brand Guardian runs the grep rules from `docs/design/brand-system.md` §2 on the proposed KOSMOS tree and finds zero matches in production source. Every token name in `tui/src/theme/tokens.ts` type surface matches the `{metaphorRole}{Variant}?` pattern or is on the CC-legacy allow-list (SC-002).

### Implementation for User Story 2

- [X] T019 [US2] Author `docs/design/brand-system.md` §2 Token naming doctrine (≥ 500 words per SC-003 / BSS-03) covering: grammar (BNF from token-naming-grammar.md §1), BAN-01..BAN-07 table with error messages (BSS-08), exceptions (semantic-safety keywords + CC-legacy allow-list convention), ministry roster pointer to §1, Brand Guardian review contract, three rejection worked examples (`primary`, `accent1`, `claudeShimmer` — SC-012), future-proofing process
- [X] T020 [US2] Audit `tui/src/theme/tokens.ts` type surface — verify every NEW identifier added under this Epic matches `{metaphorRole}{Variant}?` grammar and passes BAN-01..BAN-07; do NOT rename existing CC-legacy identifiers (that is Deferred row 10, out of scope per FR-030); append additive tokens only if required by catalog examples (most Epics require zero additions — this Task may complete as a no-op with an audit summary comment)

**Checkpoint**: Brand Guardian rejection pathway live. SC-012 test passes: a simulated ad-hoc proposal can be rejected citing a single §2 subsection.

---

## Phase 5: User Story 3 — Cross-Epic Design Consistency via Single Source of Truth (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Deliver `docs/design/brand-system.md` as the full 10-section canonical document that every design-concerned Epic cites — §1 authored by this Epic, §3–§10 reserved as placeholders with scope-violation protection.

**Independent Test**: A reviewer opens any Epic H/J/K/L/E spec input (once they enter Spec Kit) and finds an explicit "References `docs/design/brand-system.md`" statement, AND finds §1 fully authored AND §3–§10 placeholders each ≤ 50 words with `Owner: Epic X #NNNN` pointer.

### Implementation for User Story 3

- [X] T021 [US3] Scaffold `docs/design/brand-system.md` with 10 H2 section headings (`## §1` … `## §10`) in order per BSS-01 + brand-system-sections.md §1, and include the document title `# KOSMOS Brand System` with no preamble text (BSS-09)
- [X] T022 [US3] Author §1 Brand metaphor (≥ 500 words per SC-003 / BSS-02) covering the five required content areas from brand-system-sections.md §2: KOSMOS 은하계 integration metaphor (BSS-07 includes literal strings `KOSMOS` + `은하계` + ministry roster header), visual element vocabulary, ministry satellite roster (KOROAD/KMA/HIRA/NMC/119/Geocoding), why metaphor matters for text UI, cross-references to ADR-006 A-9 + assets/kosmos-*.svg + Korea AI Action Plan Principle 8/9
- [X] T023 [US3] Populate §3–§10 placeholders (≤ 50 words each per BSS-04/BSS-05) using the exact template from brand-system-sections.md §4 — owner pointer per BSS-06: §3–§7 + §9 → `Owner: Epic H #1302`; §8 → `Owners: Epic H #1302 + Epic K #1308`; §10 → `Owners: all design-concerned Epics (B/C/D/E/H/I/J/K/L/M)` with the dated-H3-subheading append convention note
- [X] T024 [US3] Validate `docs/design/brand-system.md` against BSS-01..BSS-09 (ten H2 sections in order, §1 ≥ 500 words, §2 ≥ 500 words, §3–§10 each ≤ 50 words, §3–§10 each contain `Owner:` line, §1 contains `KOSMOS` + `은하계`, §2 contains `BAN-01`..`BAN-07`, no preamble between title and §1) — fix any violations in place

**Checkpoint**: Brand system doctrine ships. §3–§10 scope-violation trap armed for `/speckit-analyze` (FR-034).

## Phase 6: User Story 4 — Accessibility Auditor Gates Each Verdict (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Deliver `docs/tui/accessibility-gate.md` so every PORT/REWRITE verdict propagates its WCAG 2.1 AA subset + KWCAG 2.2 notes + IME flag + contrast constraint to the owning Epic's Tasks.

**Independent Test**: A reviewer opens `docs/tui/accessibility-gate.md` and, for every PORT/REWRITE verdict in the catalog, finds a matching row with required WCAG criteria (⊆ `{1.4.3, 2.1.1, 2.4.7, 3.3.2, 4.1.2}`) and any Korean-specific gate note (SC-009: 0 orphan verdicts).

### Implementation for User Story 4

- [X] T025 [US4] Author `docs/tui/accessibility-gate.md` header per accessibility-gate-rows.md §1 (Epic, WCAG baseline 2.1 AA subset, KWCAG 2.2 baseline for citizen-facing, FR-022 palette handoff to Epic H #1302 ≥ 4.5:1 body / ≥ 3:1 large text, IME composition rule pointer to Epic E #1300)
- [X] T026 [P] [US4] Generate one row per PORT/REWRITE catalog row from Phase 3 — emit rows for design-system + IO + permission families (grouped to minimize table churn) with columns per accessibility-gate-rows.md §3; at minimum every visible component has `1.4.3` in its WCAG set
- [X] T027 [P] [US4] Populate `KWCAG notes` column for citizen-facing families per accessibility-gate-rows.md §4 (`PromptInput`, `messages`, `Settings`, `root.onboarding`, `HelpV2`, any row targeting `tui/src/components/conversation/` or `tui/src/components/input/`, plus `Passes`, `permissions`, `Spinner` in citizen-visible flows)
- [X] T028 [US4] Flag `IME-safe = yes` for every row whose CC component matches accessibility-gate-rows.md §5 heuristics (useState<string> + Ink `<TextInput>`/`<BaseTextInput>`, onSubmit/onChange with string payload, `PromptInput/` + `CustomSelect/` with search-filter); for each IME-safe row, ensure the paired `TaskSubIssue` body (materialized by `/speckit-taskstoissues`) will carry the acceptance-checklist line `- [ ] All keyboard handlers gated on !useKoreanIME().isComposing before mutating input buffer`
- [X] T029 [US4] Validate `docs/tui/accessibility-gate.md` against AG-01..AG-06 (every PORT/REWRITE row has a paired gate row; WCAG non-empty; citizen-facing KWCAG non-empty; IME-safe composition-gate acceptance line present; Contrast values ⊆ `{4.5:1, 3:1, n/a}`; WCAG values ⊆ closed set of 5) — fix in place; cross-link catalog rows to gate rows via the `Accessibility gate` column anchors to satisfy I6

**Checkpoint**: Every PORT/REWRITE verdict carries an accessibility contract to its owning Epic. Epic H #1302 inherits the palette contrast constraint via the header.

## Phase 7: User Story 5 — Task Sub-Issue Traceability (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Convert every REWRITE verdict into a Task sub-issue under the owning Epic via Sub-Issues API v2 while staying within Epic M's 90-cap (FR-025) and correctly distributing non-M Tasks to their owning Epics (FR-026).

**Independent Test**: A GraphQL query enumerates all REWRITE rows in the merged catalog and verifies each has a corresponding Task sub-issue under its owning Epic (B/C/D/E/H/I/J/K/L/M), AND Epic M's sub-issue count (excluding `[Deferred]`-prefixed) ≤ 90 (SC-004, SC-007).

### Implementation for User Story 5

- [X] T030 [US5] Register all 6 `NEEDS TRACKING` Deferred items (rows 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 16 from spec.md Deferred table) as `[Deferred]`-prefixed Tasks under Epic M per Constitution §VI — prefix excludes them from the 90-cap per FR-025; one of these is `[Deferred] Implement Brand Guardian grep CI gate workflow` tied to `contracts/grep-gate-rules.md`
- [X] T031 [US5] Run `/speckit-taskstoissues` to materialize the REWRITE + family-batch + DEFER + process Tasks as GitHub issues linked via `addSubIssue` mutation per FR-023 + research §R4 allocation; verify via `gh api graphql` (AGENTS.md GraphQL-only rule): Epic M `subIssues.totalCount` (excluding `[Deferred]` prefix) ≤ 90, each non-M owning Epic receives its own REWRITE Tasks (FR-026), closed-Epic (B #1297 / A #1298) REWRITEs are re-parented to Epic M per research §R3
- [X] T032 [US5] Backfill the `Task sub-issue` column in `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` with each row's materialized `#<number>` (or `—` for PORT rows marked "implementation complete" and for aggregated-row constituents); verify SC-004 (100% REWRITE coverage) via row-by-row grep

**Checkpoint**: Complete issue-graph traceability. Every REWRITE verdict has a GitHub Task sub-issue.

## Phase 8: User Story 6 — DISCARD Evidence Integrity (Priority: P3)

**Goal**: Every DISCARD row cites ADR-006 Part D-1, Part D-3, or a specific domain-mismatch rationale — unjustified DISCARDs fail validation (SC-005).

**Independent Test**: Open `docs/tui/component-catalog.md`, grep rows where `Verdict = DISCARD`, confirm every row's `Rationale` starts with one of `ADR-006 Part D-1`, `ADR-006 Part D-3`, or `Domain mismatch:` (invariant I4).

### Implementation for User Story 6

- [X] T033 [US6] Audit every DISCARD row's `Rationale` column in `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` against invariant I4: starts with one of the three allowed prefixes. For each violation, either (a) append the missing ADR-006 Part D section reference from the T006 evidence index, or (b) re-classify to REWRITE/DEFER if the DISCARD is unsupported. Document audit result in the catalog trailer with row numbers checked
- [X] T034 [US6] Run `/speckit-analyze` against the committed tree and resolve any reported violations (I3–I17, BSS-01..BSS-09, AG-01..AG-06); `/speckit-analyze` output recorded in `specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/analyze-report.md` (scratch) with all PASS gates green

**Checkpoint**: All four deliverables (component-catalog, accessibility-gate, brand-system, tokens.ts) pass invariants. PR is ready for review.

## Phase 9: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

**Purpose**: Final consistency checks, agent-context refresh, and PR wiring.

- [X] T035 [P] Run `.specify/scripts/bash/update-agent-context.sh claude` to refresh the "Active Technologies" block in `CLAUDE.md` with "N/A — documentation-only Epic (034-tui-component-catalog)" so auto-memory reflects the shipped state
- [X] T036 [P] Readability spot-check for SC-008: pick 5 random CC component files, time how long it takes a reviewer (unfamiliar with CC) to locate verdict + owning Epic + rationale using only `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` + ADR-006 + `.references/claude-code-sourcemap/`; target < 30 min total for all 5 picks; record in analyze-report.md
- [ ] T037 Final PR preparation: confirm PR body uses `Closes #1310` only (Epic M), NOT any Task sub-issue numbers (per AGENTS.md § PR closing rule); monitor CI via `gh pr checks --watch --interval 10` until green; Copilot + Codex review gate passes

---

## Dependencies & Execution Order

### Phase Dependencies

- **Setup (Phase 1, T001–T004)**: No dependencies — can start immediately. T003 + T004 parallel.
- **Foundational (Phase 2, T005–T008)**: Depends on Phase 1. T005 → T006/T007/T008 sequential on shared scratch artifact conventions but each reads different CC/ADR/Epic inputs so may interleave; keep sequential to avoid classification drift.
- **User Stories (Phases 3–8)**: All depend on Foundational completion.
  - US1 (Phase 3) is MVP — ship after T018 if needed.
  - US2 + US3 (Phases 4–5) can start once T005 + T006 + T007 complete (parallel with US1 if team capacity).
  - US4 (Phase 6) depends on US1 PORT/REWRITE row list existing (T010–T014 completed).
  - US5 (Phase 7) depends on US1 REWRITE row list + US2 + US3 + US4 all merged to prevent orphan issue creation.
  - US6 (Phase 8) depends on US1 DISCARD row list (T010–T014).
- **Polish (Phase 9)**: Depends on US1 + US2 + US3 + US4 + US5 + US6 complete.

### User Story Dependencies

- **US1 (P1 — catalog)**: Independent of US2–US6; MVP boundary.
- **US2 (P1 — token doctrine §2 + tokens.ts audit)**: Independent of US1; may run fully parallel.
- **US3 (P1 — brand-system.md scaffold + §1 + §3–§10 placeholders)**: Independent of US1/US2; may run fully parallel.
- **US4 (P2 — accessibility-gate)**: REQUIRES US1 row list (T014 completed) to generate paired rows.
- **US5 (P2 — task sub-issue materialization)**: REQUIRES US1 REWRITE rows (T018 validated) and does NOT touch US2–US4 directly but runs after US4 so `/speckit-taskstoissues` can include accessibility-derived checklists.
- **US6 (P3 — DISCARD evidence audit)**: REQUIRES US1 DISCARD rows (T014 completed).

### Within Each User Story

- Header tasks (T009, T021, T025) before body-row tasks (T010–T014, T022–T023, T026–T028).
- Body-row tasks before validation tasks (T018, T024, T029, T033, T034).
- US5 runs `/speckit-taskstoissues` only after US1 + US4 are structurally complete (T032 backfills catalog column after T031 mutates GitHub).

### Parallel Opportunities

- **Phase 1**: T003 + T004 parallel (different scratch files, different tools).
- **Phase 3**: T010 + T011 + T012 + T013 parallel (different family subsets of the 31 subdirectories; each writes a different section of `docs/tui/component-catalog.md` — coordinate via sequential merge at T014 which also handles root files). **Note**: parallel writes to the same file require coordination; if using Agent Teams, have each Sonnet teammate emit rows to a staging fragment (`specs/034-tui-component-catalog/artifacts/catalog-fragment-<family-group>.md`) and have T014 stitch them into the final document.
- **Phase 4 + Phase 5 + Phase 6**: fully parallel across user stories once Foundational is done (three different files — `brand-system.md`, `tokens.ts`, `accessibility-gate.md`).
- **Phase 6**: T026 + T027 parallel (different column responsibilities per row — one emits core WCAG set, the other populates KWCAG notes; coordinate via T028 + T029 sequential merge).
- **Phase 9**: T035 + T036 parallel (different outputs; one is a script run, the other is a manual review exercise).

---

## Parallel Example: Phase 3 User Story 1 (Agent Teams)

```bash
# Foundational complete → fan out Sonnet teammates across family groups:
Team A (Sonnet): T010 — design-system / chrome families → catalog-fragment-chrome.md
Team B (Sonnet): T011 — conversation / IO families → catalog-fragment-io.md
Team C (Sonnet): T012 — permission / safety families → catalog-fragment-safety.md
Team D (Sonnet): T013 — tooling / integration families → catalog-fragment-tooling.md

# Lead (Opus) merges fragments and handles root-level files + aggregation + validation:
Lead: T014 + T015 + T016 + T017 + T018
```

## Parallel Example: Phases 4 + 5 (Design-doc sprint)

```bash
# Foundational complete → three concurrent documents:
Team X (Sonnet): T019 — brand-system.md §2 doctrine
Team Y (Sonnet): T021 + T022 — brand-system.md scaffold + §1 metaphor
Team Z (Sonnet): T020 — tokens.ts type-surface audit
# Lead (Opus) merges all three branches and runs T023 + T024 validation
```

---

## Implementation Strategy

### MVP First (User Story 1 only)

1. Complete Phase 1: Setup (T001–T004)
2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational (T005–T008) — CRITICAL
3. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1 (T009–T018) — catalog shipped
4. **STOP and VALIDATE**: SC-001 passes (389 rows, one verdict each); SC-005 passes (DISCARD evidence intact).
5. Ship as Epic M MVP PR; defer US2–US6 to follow-up PRs if timeline demands.

### Recommended Incremental Delivery (single PR, sequential stories)

1. Setup + Foundational → foundation ready.
2. US1 → catalog committed.
3. US2 + US3 in parallel (different files) → brand-system doctrine + metaphor shipped.
4. US4 → accessibility gate shipped (paired with US1 rows).
5. US5 → `/speckit-taskstoissues` materializes Task sub-issues under respective Epics.
6. US6 → DISCARD audit + `/speckit-analyze` final pass.
7. Polish → PR finalized, CI green, reviewers notified.

### Agent Teams Parallel Strategy

With 4+ Sonnet teammates available:

1. Lead (Opus) completes Phase 1 + Phase 2 (foundational single-author discipline).
2. Fan-out at T010–T013 (4 family-group classification Tasks) — Teams A/B/C/D emit fragments in parallel.
3. Lead stitches fragments (T014–T018).
4. Fan-out for Phases 4–5–6: Teams X (T019), Y (T021+T022), Z (T020) in parallel with Team W (T025+T026+T027+T028).
5. Lead merges, runs T024 + T029 validation, then executes T030–T032 (US5 requires sequential issue-graph mutations).
6. Lead closes with T033–T037.

---

## Sub-Issue Budget Projection (FR-025, SC-007, research §R4)

Hard cap: Epic M Task sub-issues ≤ 90 (excluding `[Deferred]`-prefixed).

**Projected allocation** (reconciled at T016 pre-flight):

| Category | Projected | Cap | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| REWRITE Tasks owned by Epic M (incl. closed-Epic re-parents from B/A per §R3) | ≤ 40 | 40 | Aggregated per FR-027 where family has > 10 REWRITEs |
| Family-batch REWRITE Tasks (FR-027) across non-M Epics that fall back to M | ≤ 30 | 30 | Covers high-density families whose owning Epic is also M |
| DEFER investigation Tasks (unblock-condition research) | ≤ 10 | 10 | Root-level DEFER rows with unblock-when conditions |
| Process Tasks (grep-gate impl, §10 collab tracker) | ≤ 10 | 10 | Grep gate impl is `[Deferred]`-prefixed so does NOT count — leaves ~10 slot headroom for real process Tasks |
| **Epic M total (non-`[Deferred]`)** | **≤ 85** | **90** | **5-slot buffer — T016 enforces re-aggregation if projection exceeds 85** |
| REWRITE Tasks owned by non-M Epics (C/D/E/H/I/J/K/L) | open | — | Per FR-026 — each Epic absorbs its own; NOT counted against M's 90-cap |
| `[Deferred]`-prefixed follow-ups on Epic M | open | — | Registered per Constitution §VI; excluded from FR-025 per spec wording |

**Total Task count in tasks.md**: 37 Tasks (well under the 90-cap; leaves ~53 slots for post-merge Task expansion).

---

## Notes

- **`[P]` Tasks = different files / different subdirectory partitions / different tools**. Tasks mutating the same document file MUST be serialized or use the staging-fragment pattern (Phase 3 Agent Teams example).
- **`[Story]` label** maps task to US1–US6 for traceability; Setup + Foundational + Polish phases intentionally omit the label per the format spec.
- **Every user story is independently testable** via the Independent Test line in that phase's header, mapped directly to the SC-NNN success criteria in spec.md.
- **Validation is embedded in each story's final Task** (T018 for US1, T024 for US3, T029 for US4, T033+T034 for US6) — no separate Phase-9 validation task needed because `/speckit-analyze` runs against the full tree at T034.
- **No pytest additions** — this Epic is documentation-only (plan.md §Technical Context confirms); `/speckit-analyze` consumes `contracts/*.md` invariants as lint rules.
- **Commit discipline**: commit after each Task or logical Task group (e.g., all four parallel classification Tasks in Phase 3 commit together once Lead merges fragments). Every commit message references the Task ID and the invariant it advances (e.g., `docs(034): T010 classify chrome families (I3 toward 389)`).
- **Avoid**: filling §3–§10 brand-system content (FR-034 scope violation), renaming CC-legacy tokens (Deferred row 10), modifying `tui/src/components/` source files (FR-030), touching `src/kosmos/**` (FR-031 + FR-033).
- **`[Deferred]` prefix rule**: Tasks registered at T030 (rows 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 16 from spec.md) MUST use the literal `[Deferred]` prefix in their GitHub issue title so FR-025 exclusion logic works. Non-`[Deferred]` Tasks count against the 90-cap.
- **Closed-Epic handling**: B #1297 and A #1298 are CLOSED (research §2.3). Any REWRITE verdict whose logical owner is B or A is re-parented to Epic M at T031 per research §R3; the catalog owner column still cites `B #1297 (closed)` / `A #1298 (closed)` for traceability.
